headline: Financial Exchanges & Data Stocks Q1 Teardown: S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) Vs The Rest
summary: As the craze of earnings season draws to a close, here’s a look back at some of the most exciting (and some less so) results from Q1. Today, we are looking at financial exchanges & data stocks, starting with S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI).

headline: Momentum ETFs Are Actually Holding Up
summary: The iShares USA Momentum Factor ETF is up 0.3%, while the while the Invesco Momentum ETF was moving in and out of positive territory.  Momentum ETFs like the iShares fund have actually added energy stocks, which were rising in the wake of the latest wave of re-escalation in the Iran war.  Chip stocks, many of which were actually up on the day, are also major players in momentum ETFs.

headline: What You Actually Pay To Join The CAT Stock Run
summary: The heavy-equipment giant is on a historic run, but for investors sizing it up today, the real question is whether the engine has more power than the price tag.

headline: The Cash Machine The Market Put On Sale: Ford
summary: The automaker is offering a cash yield that dwarfs the market average, but investors are hesitating for reasons buried in the balance sheet.

headline: S&P 9,000? Jay Hatfield explains his massive call
summary: Infrastructure Capital Advisors CEO and CIO Jay Hatfield breaks down the math behind his S&P 9,000 price target with Trader Talk host Kenny Polcari, explaining why corporate tax rates and earnings estimates support the aggressive climb despite AI valuation concerns and the upcoming midterm elections.

headline: JP Morgan Maintains Overweight on S&P Global, Lowers Price Target to $535
summary: JP Morgan  analyst Andrew Steinerman   maintains S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) with a Overweight and lowers the price target from $550 to $535.
